articles.title: Google stopped a zero-day hack that it says was developed with AI
articles.why: Google detecting and stopping a zero-day exploit that was itself built with AI is the first confirmed instance of the AI-vs-AI cyberwar scenario — not theoretical anymore, it happened, and Google stopped it this week.
articles.hook: Hackers built an exploit using AI. / Google caught it with AI. / First time ever.

articles.title: Here’s what Mira Murati’s AI company is up to
articles.why: Mira Murati — the OpenAI CTO who was briefly fired alongside Altman — unveiling her first major technical concept at Thinking Machines reveals what the most credentialed OpenAI defector actually believes the next phase of AI should look like.
articles.hook: The woman who almost took over OpenAI / just showed her hand.

articles.title: ‘The Lord of the Rings: The Rings of Power’ Season 3 Sets Fall Premiere Date
articles.why: Rings of Power Season 3 locking in a November 11 premiere date — after a divisive second season — is a meaningful content calendar moment for Amazon's most expensive franchise bet, arriving during upfront week.
articles.hook: Rings of Power Season 3 lands November 11. / Amazon's most expensive bet / gets one more shot.

articles.title: Death Row Producer Claims Tupac Shakur Estate Owes Him Unpaid ‘All Eyez on Me’ Royalties
articles.why: The day after 'All Eyez on Me' enters the Grammy Hall of Fame, a Death Row producer surfaces claiming unpaid royalties from the same album — a timely complication to the celebration that reveals how unresolved the business of Tupac's legacy still is.
articles.hook: They just inducted it into the Hall of Fame. / Now someone's suing for the money.

articles.title: Seth Meyers Skewers CBS for Being ‘So in the Pocket for Trump’ at NBC Event
articles.why: Seth Meyers roasting CBS at NBC's own upfront — for settling its Trump defamation suit — is a rare moment of industry self-reckoning done publicly, in the room where rivals are supposed to behave.
articles.hook: He roasted CBS. / At NBC's event. / About the Trump lawsuit.

articles.title: Testing for ‘Bad Cholesterol’ Doesn’t Tell the Whole Story
articles.why: As longevity metrics like VO2 max go mainstream, a rigorous challenge to standard LDL cholesterol testing — and the better alternative that most doctors still don't use — is the kind of actionable health story that lands right now.
articles.hook: Your cholesterol test is incomplete. / There's a better one. / Most doctors skip it.

articles.title: The FCC Received Hundreds of Complaints About Bad Bunny’s ‘Vulgar’ Super Bowl Performance
articles.why: WIRED obtaining the actual FCC complaint files reveals the dual nature of the backlash — not just prudishness, but anxiety about a Spanish-language performance on the biggest American broadcast stage, which is a real cultural document.
articles.hook: Hundreds complained about Bad Bunny's Super Bowl set. / Half of them were mad it was in Spanish.

articles.title: There’s an Unhinged New Video Game About Trump and the Iran War
articles.why: An anonymous artist collective building a playable video game simulating impossible Iran war decisions is a creative intervention that cuts through the noise — it's the kind of artifact that makes a geopolitical story feel real and immediate.
articles.hook: Someone made a video game about the Iran war. / It's unhinged. / It's also kind of brilliant.

articles.title: A New Hantavirus Vaccine Is in the Works
articles.why: With hantavirus passengers now in U.S. biocontainment and public anxiety peaking, the news that Moderna has been quietly developing an mRNA hantavirus vaccine since 2023 is exactly the forward-looking signal readers need right now.
articles.hook: Moderna has been working on a hantavirus vaccine. / Since 2023. / Nobody noticed until now.

articles.title: Yarbo says it will remove the intentional backdoor from its robot lawn mower
articles.why: Yarbo committing to fully remove the remote backdoor from its robot mower — after a journalist was run over and a hacker demonstrated full remote control — is a meaningful accountability moment in consumer robotics.
articles.hook: The robot that ran over a journalist / is getting its backdoor removed. / Finally.

articles.title: Palantir’s true believers are wearing this jacket
articles.why: Palantir selling a chore coat to its own believers — at a moment when the company is deeply embedded in ICE and defense contracts — is a vivid cultural artifact about how surveillance capitalism builds identity and loyalty among its own workforce.
articles.hook: Palantir is selling merch now. / A chore coat for true believers. / This is where we are.

articles.title: Bravo is creating unscripted microdramas for the Peacock app
articles.why: Peacock and Bravo launching unscripted microdramas is the moment a format that's been quietly generating billions in Asian and niche Western markets gets absorbed into mainstream American media infrastructure — a format shift, not just a new show.
articles.hook: Bravo is making microdramas now. / The format generating billions in Asia / just went mainstream.

articles.title: Digg tries again, this time as an AI news aggregator
articles.why: Digg relaunching as an AI news aggregator is a culturally loaded moment — the original Digg was the internet's first great experiment in crowd-curated news, and its rebirth as an AI curation tool captures exactly where media discovery is heading.
articles.hook: Digg is back. This time the algorithm picks. / We'll see how that goes.

articles.title: Oil prices extend gains as Trump comments diminish hopes for a U.S.-Iran peace deal
articles.why: Trump describing the Iran ceasefire as on 'life support' is the clearest signal yet that the conflict is not winding down — oil markets are responding immediately, and the economic ripple effects are accelerating.
articles.hook: Trump says the ceasefire is on 'life support.' / Oil just went up again.

articles.title: Trump invites Elon Musk, Tim Cook, Larry Fink and other CEOs to join China trip for Xi summit
articles.why: Trump bringing Musk, Cook, and Fink to Beijing for the Xi summit is an unusual blurring of corporate and diplomatic power — the world's most consequential geopolitical meeting is now also a CEO summit, with implications for AI governance, trade, and Taiwan.
articles.hook: Musk, Cook, and Fink are going to Beijing. / Trump's taking the CEOs to meet Xi.
